last_item: Roles & Databases — the per-row 'Rotate password' button rotated immediately on a single unguarded click (RolesDatabases.tsx:230 onClick called rotate() directly). Rotating invalidates the old password server-side instantly, so any live app on the old credential loses DB access until reconfigured — the SAME blast radius as the row's Delete button, which IS gated by a TypedConfirmDialog with consequence copy. The page header even promises 'Every action here is guided and confirmed', yet Rotate was the lone unguarded mutation. Surfaced by the iter-18 Roles/Backups convergence agent (the other 4 of 5 agents converged); verified against the code, then promoted to Mode F as a genuine defect/asymmetry, not decoration.
last_result: SHIPPED (4 SHIP, zero blockers — incl. restraint critic). Gated rotate behind the existing PLAIN ConfirmDialog (NOT the typed-name gate — rotation isn't data loss, so a proportionate 'are you sure?', tone=danger): adds a `rotateTarget` state mirroring `dropTarget`, button onClick now `setRotateTarget(name)`, dialog message 'The current password for <name> stops working immediately. Any app connecting as this user will lose access until you update its connection string with the new password — shown once, right after.' Removed the now-redundant inline 'Rotating…' button spinner (busy shows on the dialog Confirm). Reused the existing component, ~20 lines, +1 click. Added a test: clicking the row button does NOT call rotatePassword; only the dialog confirm does → then the one-time secrets modal appears. Panel: UX heuristics (fixes a simultaneous Consistency + Error-Prevention failure; plain confirm is the right altitude vs typed gate); Sam ('that one sentence stops me cold in the right way — saves me from nuking my live app'); Priya ('proportionate gate on an irreversible, connection-breaking op; plain confirm not type-the-name is the right call; one click, buries nothing'); restraint critic ('creates the absent FIRST gate on an unguarded production-breaking action whose sibling of equal blast radius is already gated — categorically different from the prior Nth-redundant-warning rejects; genuine error-prevention'). Gates: typecheck OK, 143 tests OK (142 + 1 new), build OK (dist regenerated + staged), go build OK (outside sandbox). stable_streak stays 0 (shipped a real improvement). LESSON: convergence is provisional AGAIN — 4 of 5 agents converged but the 5th found a real defect; an UNguarded destructive-by-effect action is the opposite of the rejected 'restating an existing warning' pattern — adding the FIRST guard clears restraint easily, esp. when a sibling action of equal blast radius is already gated and the page promises 'every action confirmed'. Next iteration: run a fresh discovery/convergence pass (streak back to 0).
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn -- rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres -- minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
